content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 29, 2006

Alexis R. Wierbinski, Chair
Committee to Reform Democracy/California
2437 Piedmont Avenue, #202
Berkeley, CA 94704

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-045

Dear Ms. Wierbinski:

This letter is in response to your request for assistance on behalf of the Committee to Reform Democracy which was received by our office on March 15, 2006. Your inquiry concerns a proposed ballot measure that seeks to amend the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

While we appreciate your interest in reforming the California election process, we have determined that it is not appropriate to advise you regarding the substance of your proposed measure therefore, your request is withdrawn. However, we may respond to questions regarding the current Political Reform Act. Your letter does not ask any specific questions concerning the Act.

If you have any specific questions concerning the current Political Reform Act, you may contact our offices at (916) 322-5660 for assistance.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-045

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: It is not appropriate to advise regarding the substance of the proposed measure; therefore, the request is withdrawn. However, the FPPC may respond to questions regarding the current Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for FPPC advice on a proposed ballot measure seeking to amend the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice on the substance of the proposed ballot measure amendment but indicated it could address specific questions about the current Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The substantive outcome (withdrawal) and reasoning are found in the body of the letter. No specific legal questions about current law were posed by the requestor.

CONCLUSION: It is not appropriate to advise regarding the substance of the proposed measure; therefore, the request is withdrawn. However, the FPPC may respond to questions regarding the current Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew a request for advice from the Committee to Reform Democracy regarding a proposed ballot measure to amend the Political Reform Act, determining it was not appropriate to advise on the measure's substance.
